name: Kung Pao Chicken #2
recipeIngredient: 19 ounces Bamboo shoots, drained and cubed
3 To 5 dried chili peppers, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into cubes.
2. Beat egg in a medium bowl; add chicken. Sprinkle with 1 1/2 tablespoons cornstarch; mix well.
3. Stir in 4 tablespoons soy and peanut oil. Marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes.
4. Mix 2 teaspoons cornstarch and the water in a small bowl until smooth. Stir in 3 tablespoons soy, the rice wine, vinegar, sugar, and sesame oil.
5. Heat wok over high heat for 15 seconds; add 3 tablespoons oil and heat until hot. Reduce heat to low.
6. Add peppers; cook, stirring and pressing them against the sides of the wok, until dark red, about 10 seconds.
7. Add ginger and garlic, stir fry for 10 seconds.
8. Increase heat to high. Scatter in chicken, about 1/4 at a time; stir fry for 1 minute after all chicken has been added.
9. Add bamboo shoots, cook for 1 minute.
10. Add scallions, cook for 30 seconds.
11. Stir cornstarch mixture; add to wok. Cook and stir until sauce thickens and coats the chicken evenly.
12. Add peanuts and turn off heat. Stir to blend.
